verses.translation: 'Your wings will grow back — do not worry. You shall point out Sita's location to them.' The sage's words have come true today. Hear my words and accomplish the Lord's work.

verses.original:
गिरि त्रिकूट ऊपर बस लंका । तहँ रह रावन सहज असंका ।।
तहँ असोक उपबन जहँ रहई ।। सीता बैठि सोच रत अहई।।
verses.transliteration:
giri trikūṭa ūpara basa laṁkā, tahaṁ raha rāvana sahaja asaṁkā.
tahaṁ asoka upabana jahaṁ rahaī, sītā baiṭhi soca rata ahaī.
verses.translation: 'Atop Mount Trikuta lies Lanka, where Ravana dwells, naturally fearless. There, in the Ashoka garden, Sita sits, absorbed in sorrow.'

verses.original:
मैं देखउँ तुम्ह नाहि गीघहि दष्टि अपार।।
बूढ भयउँ न त करतेउँ कछुक सहाय तुम्हार।।28।।
verses.transliteration:
maiṁ dekhauṁ tumha nāhi gīghahi daṣṭi apāra,
būṛha bhayauṁ na ta karateuṁ kachuka sahāya tumhāra. 28.
verses.translation: 'I can see her but you cannot, for a vulture's sight is limitless. I have grown old — otherwise I would have rendered you some help myself.'

verses.original:
जो नाघइ सत जोजन सागर । करइ सो राम काज मति आगर ।।
मोहि बिलोकि धरहु मन धीरा । राम कृपाँ कस भयउ सरीरा।।
verses.transliteration:
jo nāghai sata jojana sāgara, karai so rāma kāja mati āgara.
mohi biloki dharahu mana dhīrā, rāma kṛpāṁ kasa bhayaū sarīrā.
verses.translation: 'Whoever can leap across the ocean of a hundred yojanas — that repository of wisdom shall accomplish Rama's task. Look at me and take courage in your hearts — see how by Rama's grace my body has been restored.'

verses.original:
पापिउ जा कर नाम सुमिरहीं। अति अपार भवसागर तरहीं।।
तासु दूत तुम्ह तजि कदराई। राम हृदयँ धरि करहु उपाई।।
verses.transliteration:
pāpiu jā kara nāma sumirahīṁ, ati apāra bhavasāgara tarahīṁ.
tāsu dūta tumha taji kadarāī, rāma hṛdayaṁ dhari karahu upāī.
verses.translation: 'Even sinners who merely remember His name cross the boundless ocean of worldly existence. You are the messengers of that very Lord — give up cowardice, hold Rama in your hearts, and find a way.'

verses.original:
अस कहि गरुड़ गीध जब गयऊ। तिन्ह कें मन अति बिसमय भयऊ।।
निज निज बल सब काहूँ भाषा। पार जाइ कर संसय राखा।।
verses.transliteration:
asa kahi garuḍa gīdha jaba gayaū, tinha keṁ mana ati bisamaya bhayaū.
nija nija bala saba kāhūṁ bhāṣā, pāra jāi kara saṁsaya rākhā.
verses.translation: Having said this, the great vulture departed. The monkeys were filled with wonder. Each one declared his own strength, but all expressed doubt about being able to cross to the other side.

verses.original:
जरठ भयउँ अब कहइ रिछेसा। नहिं तन रहा प्रथम बल लेसा।।
जबहिं त्रिबिक्रम भए खरारी। तब मैं तरुन रहेउँ बल भारी।।
verses.transliteration:
jaraṭha bhayauṁ aba kahai richesā, nahiṁ tana rahā prathama bala lesā.
jabahiṁ tribikrama bhae kharārī, taba maiṁ taruna raheuṁ bala bhārī.
verses.translation: The king of bears, Jambavan, said, 'I have now grown old; not a trace of my former strength remains in my body. When the slayer of Khara took the Trivikrama form (as Vamana), I was young and immensely powerful.'

verses.original:
बलि बाँधत प्रभु बाढेउ सो तनु बरनि न जाई।
उभय धरी महँ दीन्ही सात प्रदच्छिन धाइ।।29।।
verses.transliteration:
bali bāṁdhata prabhu bāṛheu so tanu barani na jāī,
ubhaya dharī mahaṁ dīnhī sāta pradacchina dhāi. 29.
verses.translation: 'When the Lord grew in size to bind King Bali, His form was beyond description. I ran seven circumambulations around both the earth and the heavens.'

verses.original:
अंगद कहइ जाउँ मैं पारा। जियँ संसय कछु फिरती बारा।।
जामवंत कह तुम्ह सब लायक। पठइअ किमि सब ही कर नायक।।
verses.transliteration:
aṁgada kahai jāūṁ maiṁ pārā, jiyaṁ saṁsaya kachu phiratī bārā.
jāmavaṁta kaha tumha saba lāyaka, paṭhaia kimi saba hī kara nāyaka.
verses.translation: Angad said, 'I can go across, but I have some doubt about the return journey.' Jambavan said, 'You are fully capable, but how can we send the leader of us all?'

verses.original:
कहइ रीछपति सुनु हनुमाना। का चुप साधि रहेहु बलवाना।।
पवन तनय बल पवन समाना। बुधि बिबेक बिग्यान निधाना।।
verses.transliteration:
kahai rīchapati sunu hanumānā, kā cupa sādhi rahehu balavānā.
pavana tanaya bala pavana samānā, budhi bibeka bigyāna nidhānā.
verses.translation: The king of bears said, 'Listen, Hanuman — why do you sit in silence, O mighty one? Son of the wind, your strength equals the wind itself. You are the storehouse of wisdom, discernment, and spiritual knowledge.'

verses.original:
कवन सो काज कठिन जग माहीं। जो नहिं होइ तात तुम्ह पाहीं।।
राम काज लगि तब अवतारा। सुनतहिं भयउ पर्वताकारा।।
verses.transliteration:
kavana so kāja kaṭhina jaga māhīṁ, jo nahiṁ hoi tāta tumha pāhīṁ.
rāma kāja lagi taba avatārā, sunatahīṁ bhayaū parvatākārā.
verses.translation: 'What task is there in this world, dear son, that is too difficult for you? You were born precisely for Rama's work.' Upon hearing this, Hanuman grew to the size of a mountain.

verses.original:
कनक बरन तन तेज बिराजा। मानहु अपर गिरिन्ह कर राजा।।
सिंहनाद करि बारहिं बारा। लीलहीं नाषउँ जलनिधि खारा।।
verses.transliteration:
kanaka barana tana teja birājā, mānahu apara girinha kara rājā.
siṁhanāda kari bārahiṁ bārā, līlahīṁ nāṣauṁ jalanidhi khārā.
verses.translation: His body shone with a golden hue and blazing radiance, as if he were the king of another range of mountains. Roaring like a lion again and again, he declared, 'I shall leap across the salt ocean in mere sport!'

verses.original:
सहित सहाय रावनहि मारी। आनउँ इहाँ त्रिकूट उपारी।।
जामवंत मैं पूँछउँ तोही। उचित सिखावनु दीजहु मोही।।
verses.transliteration:
sahita sahāya rāvanahi mārī, ānauṁ ihāṁ trikūṭa upārī.
jāmavaṁta maiṁ pūṁchauṁ tohī, ucita sikhāvanu dījahu mohī.
verses.translation: 'I shall kill Ravana along with his allies and uproot Mount Trikuta and bring it here! But Jambavan, I ask you — give me proper guidance.'

verses.original:
एतना करहु तात तुम्ह जाई। सीतहि देखि कहहु सुधि आई।।
तब निज भुज बल राजिव नैना। कौतुक लागि संग कपि सेना।।
verses.transliteration:
etanā karahu tāta tumha jāī, sītahi dekhi kahahu sudhi āī.
taba nija bhuja bala rājiva nainā, kautuka lāgi saṁga kapi senā.
verses.translation: Jambavan replied, 'Do only this much, dear son — go, see Sita, and bring back the news. Then the lotus-eyed Lord Himself, with His own arm's strength and the monkey army accompanying Him for the sport of it, will do the rest.'

verses.original:
कपि सेन संग सँघारि निसिचर रामु सीतहि आनिहैं।
त्रैलोक पावन सुजसु सुर मुनि नारदादि बखानिहैं।।
जो सुनत गावत कहत समुझत परम पद नर पावई।
रघुबीर पद पाथोज मधुकर दास तुलसी गावई।।
verses.transliteration:
kapi sena saṁga saṁghāri nisicara rāmu sītahi ānihaiṁ,
trailoka pāvana sujasu sura muni nāradādi bakhānihaiṁ.
jo sunata gāvata kahata samujhata parama pada nara pāvaī,
raghubīra pada pāthoja madhukara dāsa tulasī gāvaī.
verses.translation: With the monkey army, Rama will destroy the demons and bring Sita back. His holy fame, which purifies the three worlds, will be sung by gods, sages, and Narada. Whoever hears, sings, recites, or contemplates this story attains the supreme state. Tulasidas, a bee at the lotus feet of the Hero of the Raghus, sings this glory.

verses.original:
भव भेषज रघुनाथ जसु सुनहि जे नर अरु नारि।
तिन्ह कर सकल मनोरथ सिद्ध करिहि त्रिसिरारि।।30(क)।।
नीलोत्पल तन स्याम काम कोटि सोभा अधिक।
सुनिअ तासु गुन ग्राम जासु नाम अघ खग बधिक।।30(ख)।।
verses.transliteration:
bhava bheṣaja raghunātha jasu sunahi je nara aru nāri,
tinha kara sakala manoratha siddha karihi trisirāri. 30(ka).
nīlotpala tana syāma kāma koṭi sobhā adhika,
sunia tāsu guna grāma jāsu nāma agha khaga badhika. 30(kha).
verses.translation: The fame of the Lord of the Raghus is the remedy for worldly existence. Those men and women who hear it — the slayer of Trishira (Rama) will fulfill all their desires. His body is dark as a blue lotus, His beauty surpasses that of a million Kamadevas. Hear the multitude of virtues of Him whose very name is the hunter that slays the bird of sin.
